concepts.title: Linear Programming Problem Formulation
concepts.description: A linear programming problem consists of maximizing or minimizing a linear objective function, subject to a set of linear constraints.

concepts.title: Feasible Region
concepts.description: The set of all possible solutions to a linear programming problem is called the feasible region.

concepts.title: Corner Points
concepts.description: Corner points of the feasible region are the optimal solutions to a linear programming problem.

concepts.title: Simplex Method
concepts.description: Simplex method is an algorithm used to solve linear programming problems. It involves a series of tableaux to find the optimal solution.

concepts.title: Ignoring Non-Negativity Constraints
concepts.description: Students often forget to check the non-negativity constraints of variables in a linear programming problem. This can lead to incorrect solutions.

concepts.title: Types of Linear Programming Problems
concepts.description: There are two types of linear programming problems: minimization and maximization problems.

concepts.title: Graphical Method
concepts.description: The graphical method is a visual approach to solving linear programming problems by plotting the feasible region and finding the optimal solution.

concepts.title: Shadow Price
concepts.description: The shadow price of a constraint is the rate at which the objective function increases when the right-hand side of the constraint increases by one unit.
